item.title: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 to win FIFA World Cup after 16 years
item.description: Spain have won the 2026 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 16 years, defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 after a dramatic final that went into extra time in New Jersey. The two teams were locked at 0-0 after 90 minutes, forcing the match into extra time.
item.content:encoded:
Spain have won the 2026 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 16 years, defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 after a dramatic final that went into extra time in New Jersey.

The two teams were locked at 0-0 after 90 minutes, forcing the match into extra time.

Argentina were later reduced to 10 men after Enzo Fernández was sent off.

Spain eventually found the breakthrough through Ferran Torres in the 106th minute of extra time to secure their second FIFA World Cup title and their first in 16 years.

The win also ended Argentina's reign as world champions after they claimed the title in Qatar in 2022.

More than 80,000 fans packed New York New Jersey Stadium to watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina.

The winners will also take home about FJ$112 million in prize money with t he runners-up will receive about FJ$75 million.

Meanwhile, global music stars Madonna, Shakira and Justin Bieber entertained fans during the halftime show.

Hollywood actor Tom Cruise made a special appearance and delivered a speech before kickoff.

item.title: Manoa Kamikamica's alleged perjury trial begins in Suva
item.description: Former Deputy Prime Minister, Manoa Kamikamica's trial starts this morning in the Suva Magistrates Court. He is charged with perjury and giving false information to a public servant.
item.content:encoded:
Former Deputy Prime Minister, Manoa Kamikamica's trial starts this morning in the Suva Magistrates Court.

He is charged with perjury and giving false information to a public servant.

FICAC is expected to call 4 witnesses while defence lawyer Wylie Clarke earlier indicated that he will only call 1 witness.

It is alleged that between 1st December 2024 and 31st March 2025 in Suva, Kamikamica knowingly made a false statement under oath, claiming that he had nothing to do with the appointment of the FICAC Commissioner.

It is further alleged that Kamikamica provided the same false information to the Commissioner of Inquiry (COI).

The allegations against Kamikamica stem from the Commission of Inquiry report into the appointment of Barbara Malimali as FICAC Commissioner.

Kamikamica had earlier made it clear to fijivillage News that the claims were malicious.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Savusavu vendors call for fuel price review amid vegetable shortage
item.description: Farmers and market vendors in Savusavu continue to face a shortage of fresh vegetables, with rising fuel costs as one of the main reasons behind the ongoing problem. Speaking to fijivillage News, Market Vendor Sambhu Lal says cabbage is currently the only vegetable available in large quantities, while beans, English cabbage, tomatoes and eggplants remain in short supply.
item.content:encoded:
Farmers and market vendors in Savusavu continue to face a shortage of fresh vegetables, with rising fuel costs as one of the main reasons behind the ongoing problem.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Market Vendor Sambhu Lal says cabbage is currently the only vegetable available in large quantities, while beans, English cabbage, tomatoes and eggplants remain in short supply.

Lal says rising fuel price is one of the biggest reasons behind the shortage, as fewer farmers are now growing vegetables in the Savusavu area.

He says vendors are now forced to source vegetables from Dreketi and Labasa, but the high cost of fuel makes transportation expensive, discouraging many farmers from bringing their produce to Savusavu.

Lal says the prices of other vegetables also remain high, with English cabbage selling for $5 each.

He adds that while a small number of farmers from Vilogo continue to supply the market, they are unable to meet the growing demand.

Lal is calling on the government to review fuel prices to help reduce transportation costs and support farmers, which he says will help ensure a more reliable supply of vegetables to the Savusavu Market.

For now, both vendors and consumers continue to feel the impact of the vegetable shortage and rising food prices in Savusavu.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: $400,000 investment creates jobs and improves access to spare parts in Seaqaqa
item.description: Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 19/07/2026 Three new jobs have been created in Seaqaqa following the opening of a new spare parts and hardware business aimed at making vehicle parts and hardware supplies more accessible and affordable for residents.
item.content:encoded:
Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 19/07/2026

Three new jobs have been created in Seaqaqa following the opening of a new spare parts and hardware business aimed at making vehicle parts and hardware supplies more accessible and affordable for residents.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Business Owner and Director Rajneel Chand says the business has already employed three staff members, with plans to recruit more as it expands adding he invested around $400,000 to establish the business in Seaqaqa.

Former Secondary School Mathematics and Physics teacher, Chand says he resigned from teaching to pursue a career in business.

Insert: Chand on parts store 19th July .

According to Chand, the business imports products directly from overseas, allowing it to offer lower prices while maintaining good quality.

He says the store currently stocks about 80 percent of the spare parts needed for motor vehicles, along with a wide range of hardware products at competitive prices.

In addition to spare parts and hardware, the business also operates a tyre repair centre and sells quality tyres.

Chand says the shop is conveniently located in Seaqaqa Town, making it easily accessible for customers across the surrounding areas.

The new business is expected to improve access to essential vehicle parts and hardware supplies while contributing to employment and economic activity in Seaqaqa.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Dairo remains banned during beche-de-mer season- Ministry of Fisheries
item.description: The Ministry says the beche-de-mer season will run from 1st August 2026 to 31st January 2027, but Dairo is not among the species that can be harvested. They say Dairo must not be harvested, bought, processed, exported or traded under any circumstances.
item.content:encoded:
The Ministry says the beche-de-mer season will run from 1st August 2026 to 31st January 2027, but Dairo is not among the species that can be harvested.

They say Dairo must not be harvested, bought, processed, exported or traded under any circumstances.

The Ministry is also reminding fishers and traders to comply with all requirements under the Fisheries Act.

These include obtaining the necessary licences and permits, observing minimum size limits, and avoiding the harvesting of juvenile sea cucumbers and protected species.

People are also encouraged to report any illegal harvesting or trading of Dairo or other protected species to their nearest Ministry of Fisheries office.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: From Batiki Island to international recognition in maritime law
item.description: The Maritime Safety Authority of Fiji officer has received international recognition, after graduating with a Master of Laws in International Maritime Law from the International Maritime Law Institute. Growing up on an island where the sea is central to daily life, inspired her passion for the maritime industry. She now serves at the Maritime Safety Authority of Fiji, helping to promote safe marit
item.content:encoded:
The Maritime Safety Authority of Fiji officer has received international recognition, after graduating with a Master of Laws in International Maritime Law from the International Maritime Law Institute.

Growing up on an island where the sea is central to daily life, inspired her passion for the maritime industry. She now serves at the Maritime Safety Authority of Fiji, helping to promote safe maritime transport and protect Fiji's waters.

Her achievement is a proud moment for her family and an inspiration to young people in Batiki, Lomaiviti and across Fiji , showing that determination and hard work can open doors to opportunities on the world stage.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Man driving alleged stolen vehicle arrested after police interception in Wailoku
item.description: A man allegedly driving a stolen vehicle has been arrested after police intercepted him in Wailoku, damaging a police vehicle duri
